chapter_title.ar: بَابُ الْعِلَاجِ لِلْمُحْرِمِ إِذَا مَرِضَ أَوْ أَصَابَهُ جُرْحٌ أَوْ خُرَاجٌ أَوْ عِلَّةٌ
chapter_title.en: Chapter 94 – The treatment for the one in Ihraam when he falls sick, or is hit by an injury, or pus, or a disease

gradings: Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: مجهول - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (7/319)
Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: مجهول - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (7/319)
text: سَأَلْتُهُ عَنِ الْمُحْرِمِ يُصِيبُ أُذُنَهُ الرِّيحُ فَيَخَافُ أَنْ يَمْرَضَ هَلْ يَصْلُحُ لَهُ أَنْ يَسُدَّ أُذُنَيْهِ بِالْقُطْنِ قَالَ نَعَمْ لَا بَأْسَ بِذَلِكَ إِذَا خَافَ ذَلِكَ وَ إِلَّا فَلَا .

en.hubeali: Muhammad Bin Yahya, from Muhammad Bin Ahmad, from Muhammad Bin Najiya, from Muhammad Bin Ali, from Marwan Bin Muslim, from Sama’at,

(It has been narrated) from Abu Abdullahasws, said, ‘I asked himasws about the one in Ihraam whose ears are hit by the wind, so he fears that he would fall sick. Is it correct for him that he closes his ears with the cotton?’ Heasws said: ‘Yes, there is no problem with that when he fears that, or else, so no’.
en.sarwar: 9. Muhammad ibn Yahya Ahmad ibn Muhammad from Muhammad ibn Najiyah from Muhammad ibn Ali from Marwan ibn Muslim from who has said the following: “I once asked abu ‘Abd Allah, ‘Alayhi al-Salam, about a man who is in the state of Ihram, his ears are affected by winds and is afraid of illness; if he can place cotton in his ears. He (the Imam) said, ‘It is not harmful if he is afraid of illness, otherwise, it is not permissible.’”
